key_insight: Graph neural networks trained only on finite Cayley graphs can generalize to truncated Cayley graphs of unseen infinite groups, indicating that local graph structure carries growth-related algebraic information.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a path for using learned graph representations to infer structural properties of large or infinite group-like systems from finite local samples.
method: Train and validate a GNN on finite complete and truncated Cayley graphs.
Evaluate the frozen model on truncated Cayley graphs from unseen infinite groups.
Test across free abelian groups, the discrete Heisenberg group, the infinite dihedral group, free groups, and direct products.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong zero-shot generalization from finite-group training data to multiple families of infinite finitely generated groups.
watch_item: Performance may depend on truncation depth and the extent to which finite local neighborhoods preserve the global growth behavior of the infinite group.
paper_title: From Finite Cayley Graphs to Growth of Infinite Groups
